channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump's complaints about '60 Minutes' put a spotlight on editing at the nation's top newsmagazine
item.description: Donald Trump skipped a “60 Minutes” interview this past week, but he's attacking CBS for how it handled its session with opponent Kamala Harris, calling it a “giant fake news scam” and suggesting the network was out to protect her.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The Latest: Milton causes widespread power outages and several deaths in Florida
item.description: Hurricane Milton brought powerful winds, a dangerous storm surge and flooding to much of Florida after making landfall along the Gulf Coast as a Category 3 storm. It weakened as it plowed through Florida late Wednesday into Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 1 person is dead and 12 are trapped in a former Colorado gold mine
item.description: DENVER (AP) — One person was killed and officials were working to rescue 12 others trapped deep beneath the surface of a former Colorado gold mine after an elevator malfunctioned at the tourist site, authorities said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CNN's Anderson Cooper hit by flying object during Milton live shot
item.description: CNN’s Anderson Cooper was hit by a flying object on Wednesday during a live shot while reporting from Florida on Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Penn State makes its first west coast Big Ten trip to USC
item.description: UNIVERSITY PARK (Nittany Nation) -- It's not your granddaddy's Big Ten anymore and this week's conference lineup is the people's exhibit A. Minnesota heads to UCLA, Ohio State is at Oregon, and Penn State heads to USC. Three games that hardly feel like Big Ten conference play.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Clay, NewsNation

item.title: Tampa sees 5x the usual October rainfall due to Hurricane Milton
item.description: St. Petersburg saw 15 to 18 inches of rainfall, while areas in and around Tampa saw 12 to 13 inches of rain.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, NewsNation

item.title: Tampa sees 5x the usual October rainfall due to Hurricane Milton
item.description: Hurricane Milton's landfall as a Category 3 hurricane didn't just bring devastating storm surge and extremely dangerous wind speeds, it also left Tampa Bay soaked with historic rainfall.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KREX 5

item.title: An 'appalled' New Zealand official says a navy ship didn't sink because its captain was a woman
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — New Zealand's defense minister issued stinging rebukes of what she said were “vile” and “misogynistic” online remarks by “armchair admirals” about the woman captain of a navy ship that ran aground, caught fire and sank off the coast of Samoa.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What to know about Hurricane Milton after it slammed Florida's Gulf Coast
item.description: As Hurricane Milton barreled into and across Florida, it spawned dozens of tornadoes, knocked out power to millions, damaged homes, flooded neighborhoods and led to hundreds of rescues. But the damage wasn't as bad as had been feared. Gov.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Viral Tampa sensation 'Lieutenant Dan,' who rode out Hurricane Milton in boat, describes how he survived
item.description: A Florida man named Joseph Malinowski, referred to by Tampa locals as "Lt. Dan," went viral on TikTok before his criminal background was revealed to users.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ethel Kennedy, social activist and wife of Robert F. Kennedy, has died
item.description: BOSTON, Mass. (AP) — Ethel Kennedy, the wife of Sen. Robert F. Kennedy, who raised their 11 children after he was assassinated and remained dedicated to social causes and the family’s legacy for decades thereafter, died on Thursday, her family said. She was 96.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and STEVE LeBLANC, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Internet Archive data breach exposes more than 31 million user accounts: reports
item.description: The Internet Archive, a popular digital library known for its Wayback Machine, was hacked and suffered a breach that reportedly exposed the data of 31 million user accounts.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KREX 5

item.title: Tampa woman killed by falling branch after Hurricane Milton
item.description: Investigators said that it appears that reconstruction efforts were being done in the area when the branch fell on the victim. So far, the death appears to be an accident.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, NewsNation

item.title: Immigrants brought to the US as children ask judges to keep protections against deportation
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Immigrants who built lives in the United States after being brought here illegally as children were among more than 200 demonstrators who gathered Thursday outside a federal courthouse in New Orleans, where three appellate judges heard arguments over the Biden administration's pol
item.dc:creator: KEVIN McGILL and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Chicago man charged with assaulting two officers during protests of Netanyahu address to Congress
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An Illinois man was arrested Thursday on charges that he attacked two police officers during protests in Washington, D.C., against Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s address to Congress in July.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida deputies rescue boy floating on piece of fence in floodwaters
item.description: A Florida boy floating in the flooded water in Tampa was rescued by sheriff's deputies on a patrol boat, authorities said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Delphi prepares for upcoming double murder trial of Richard Allen
item.description: Authorities will restrict access to the Carroll County courthouse during the anticipated four-week-long trial
item.dc:creator: Russ McQuaid, NewsNation

item.title: A former DEA agent is convicted of protecting drug traffickers
item.description: BUFFALO, N.Y. (AP) — A former U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration agent in Buffalo, New York, was convicted of corruption Thursday following a second trial on charges he used his position to protect drug traffickers he believed were associated with organized crime.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Indiana growers seeing above average pumpkin crops this fall
item.description: "These are some of the most beautiful pumpkins we've raised in years," said Kyle Spencer, co-owner of Spencer Farm in Noblesville.
item.dc:creator: Scarlett O’Hara, NewsNation

item.title: Pennsylvania father, sons accused of poaching nearly 50 deer
item.description: An investigation that lasted almost two years found that the Nelsons had been illegally taking whitetail bucks for several years, according to state game wardens.
item.dc:creator: Rian Bossler, NewsNation

item.title: Disney World and other Orlando parks to reopen Friday after Hurricane Milton shutdown
item.description: Florida theme parks including Disney World, Universal Orlando and SeaWorld say they’ll open their gates on Friday after shutting down for Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Mike Schneider, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Fisher-Price recalls over 2 million infant swings after 5 deaths reported
item.description: A recall has been issued for more than 2 million Fisher-Price infant swings after five deaths were reported.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, NewsNation

item.title: Biden tells Trump to 'get a life, man' and stop storm misinformation
item.description: President Joe Biden says his predecessor Donald Trump should “get a life” and try to help people impacted by a pair of devastating hurricanes, rather than spread misinformation about the federal response.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak And Will Weissert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pulpit politics: When does it cross the line?
item.description: The lead pastor at a metro Grand Rapids megachurch got political in a recent sermon, urging his parishioners to run from the Democratic party.
item.dc:creator: Ken Kolker, NewsNation

item.title: 14-year-old found floating on fence in Tampa floodwaters after Hurricane Milton
item.description: A 14-year-old had to be rescued after Hurricane Milton's floodwaters trapped him on a floating fence in the middle of Tampa.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Flesh eating bacteria thrives after hurricanes. Here's what to know
item.description: "You step on a nail, slice your leg, you get an open wound and the bacteria gets in,” said an infectious disease expert.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Over 135 rescued from Tampa assisted living facility amid 'unprecedented' flooding from Milton
item.description: “This is extraordinary to see this type of flooding, especially in this type of area,” said Hillsborough County Sheriff Chad Chronister.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CNN's Anderson Cooper hit by flying object during Milton live shot
item.description: CNN’s Anderson Cooper was hit by a flying object on Wednesday during a live shot while reporting from Florida on Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Milton knocks out power to millions and spawns slew of tornadoes. At least 5 dead
item.description: Hurricane Milton barreled into the Atlantic Ocean on Thursday after plowing across Florida, where it knocked out power to more than 3 million customers and whipped up a barrage of tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ethel Kennedy, widow of Robert F. Kennedy, suffers stroke
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The family of Ethel Kennedy, the widow of Robert F. Kennedy, says she's been hospitalized after suffering a stroke.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NC National Guard helicopter crew grounded after 'rotor wash' incident blows away Helene aid
item.description: Video of the incident appears to show the wind from the helicopter blowing over tents and scattering items in the air.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, KRON4

item.title: Ethel Kennedy, widow of Robert F. Kennedy, hospitalized after suffering stroke, family says
item.description: Ethel Kennedy, 96, is former President John F. Kennedy's sister-in-law and mother to 11 children, including former independent presidential candidate Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, ABC 4

item.title: Over 9 million pounds of ready-to-eat meat, poultry recalled: USDA
item.description: The products may be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes, according to the U.S. Department of Agriculture.
item.dc:creator: Michael Reiner, Fox 5

item.title: iPhone update allows users to text for help when there's no cell service. How it works
item.description: With Hurricane Milton barreling toward Florida's Gulf Coast, many Tampa Bay area residents are fearing widespread and potentially weeks-long power outages, which could affect cellphone service, as many saw during Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, ABC 4

item.title: Trump campaign blasts Bob Woodward; denies secret Putin calls
item.description: Donald Trump's campaign is rejecting claims in Bob Woodward's new book 'War' about the former president holding secret calls with Vladimir Putin since leaving office.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane Milton may be October surprise in presidential battle
item.description: Strategists in both parties are looking back to Hurricane Sandy, which slammed into the mid-Atlantic states shortly before Election Day 2012.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Walmart will be closed on Thanksgiving Day for 5th consecutive year
item.description: Walmart will be closed on Thanksgiving Day for the fifth year in a row, the retail giant recently announced in its holiday plans.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, WGN 9
